articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The Green Party admits that its leader has, until recently, been living on a houseboat in London and may have failed to pay council tax.
articles.title: Polanski apologises for 'unintentional mistake' over houseboat council tax

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The 28-year-old Iraqi Kurd has evaded arrest by operating under the alias “Kardo Ranya”.
articles.title: BBC unmasks key people smuggler in network behind most small boat crossings

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Every paper on Tuesday has dedicated its front page to the future of Sir Keir Starmer's premiership.
articles.title: Newspaper headlines: 'Cabinet turns on Starmer' and 'You have been murdered'

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: An American and a French national who have returned home have tested positive for hantavirus.
articles.title: Last passengers leave virus-hit cruise ship as three more test positive

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: From 12 May, only learner drivers can book their own tests, not instructors.
articles.title: How driving test booking is changing for learner drivers

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: The BBC investigation revealed how some self-described sleep experts have been giving new parents advice that goes against NHS guidelines.
articles.title: Calls for 'urgent action' on baby-sleep industry after BBC investigation

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: A dam appears to have broken, with Labour MPs posting their public loss of confidence in the PM, our political editor writes.
articles.title: Chris Mason: PM confronts his most difficult day as party revolts

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Couple whose baby was stillborn in hospital hope maternity review will lead to improvements.
articles.title: Baby loss couple: We were told we'd picked a bad day to give birth

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: Israel passes a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for those involved in the unprecedented Hamas-led attacks.
articles.title: Israel passes law to allow death penalty and public trials for those linked to October 7

articles.author: BBC News
articles.description: An Education Select Committee report finds the government needs to make urgent plans for universities facing insolvency.
articles.title: Students at risk if universities go bust, say MPs
